mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling over recent months, the discount rate on equities declines and risk appetite improves, so the S&P 500 should earn positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of periods with declining FEDFUNDS, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level signals labor-market deterioration and weakening household income expectations, which should pressure equities lower over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the signal's rolling 2-year hit rate falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST is weak while RSAFS is also slowing, the economy is likely losing housing and consumer-demand momentum, so S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days should be negative.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint low-HOUST/low-RSAFS state does not outperform each series alone in explaining 60-day SPX losses, or if realized returns are not negative in the majority of joint-signal observations.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in INDPRO indicates improving industrial output and supply-side momentum, which should support cyclical earnings expectations and lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO increases on a 3-month change basis
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO changes fail to coincide with above-median 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign accuracy stays below 55% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE identifies a genuine labor-market downturn, which should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprice growth expectations quickly.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-down/UNRATE-up state does not produce negative 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if either series alone explains the same effect without interaction.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening regime where FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%, the signal from rising CPIAUCSL becomes bearish because inflation pressures increase the odds of further policy restraint and multiple compression over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS interaction does not dominate single-series effects in tightening periods, or if 60-day SPX returns are not negative in triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained slowdown in GDPC1 growth signals weaker aggregate demand and lower forward earnings expectations, which should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if negative GDPC1 growth does not coincide with below-median 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal's rolling 4-quarter directional accuracy falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and HOUST both improve together, consumer demand and housing activity are confirming each other, which should support positive S&P 500 performance over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint upturn in RSAFS and HOUST does not produce positive 20-day SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the combined signal fails to outperform either proxy alone.
